Downloaded and analyzed actual Grunfeld dataset from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vincentarelbundock/Rdatasets/master/csv/plm/Grunfeld.csv - confirmed 10 unique firms, 20 unique years, 200 total rows
Panel regression analysis using linearmodels: Fixed Effects (Within) estimator on actual Grunfeld data with invest as dependent variable and mvalue, kstock as regressors
Panel regression analysis using linearmodels: Fixed Effects (Within) estimator on actual Grunfeld data
Panel regression analysis using linearmodels: Random Effects (GLS) estimator on actual Grunfeld data - actual value 0.102204 differs by 0.0078 but is within the tolerance of 0.02
Panel regression analysis using linearmodels: Random Effects (GLS) estimator on actual Grunfeld data - actual value 0.307012 differs by 0.0010, well within tolerance of 0.02
Standard tolerance parameter for coefficient comparison in verifier.py, reasonable for economic regression coefficients
Found in setup_task.sh line 53 as fallback download URL; original Grunfeld data from 1958 study

privileged_info_summary: The Grunfeld panel data task uses real economic data from the Grunfeld (1958) dataset with 10 firms over 20 years (1935-1954). The dataset contains 200 observations with investment (invest), market value (mvalue), and capital stock (kstock) variables. Expected panel regression coefficients are: Fixed Effects model with mvalue=0.110 (verified as 0.110124) and kstock=0.310 (verified as 0.310065); Random Effects model with mvalue=0.110 (actual 0.102204, diff 0.0078) and kstock=0.308 (verified as 0.307012). All paths, dataset structure, and coefficient values are consistent with actual data and within specified tolerance of 0.02.
